text:
Secretary, CO), Sir Oliver Robbins (former Permanent Under-Secretary of State for Foreign 
Affairs, FCDO) and others 
Subject: RE: [Do not forward/official sensitive] Draft PM letter from Cab Sec 
Date: Tuesday, 16 September 2025 at 11:25:14 British Summer Time  
From: 
To: Symes, Elkie - CO (OFF-SEN), Oliver Robbins  
CC: Ailsa Terry, Dan York-Smith, Simon Madden, Ellen Atkinson, Zoe Kinch (Cabinet Office) (Sensitive) 
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE 
Hi Elkie,  
Minor change below – we would rather not say concluded as the conflict of 
interest process is an ongoing process - it has to be updated if changes in 
interests occur. Kind regards,  
 
 Deputy Private Secretary to the 
Permanent Under Secretaries, Sir Oliver Robbins and Nick Dyer | Permanent 
Under-Secretaries’ Joint Private Office  
 | King Charles Street | Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office 
Email: 
 Mob: 
Please ensure items for the PUSs’ daily e-box reach PUS Action by 1400 clearly marked for the relevant 
PS. Our portfolios can be found here.Please note that we will not routinely keep a record of any emails 
or attachments.  
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE 
From: Elkie Symes  
Sent: Tuesday, 16 September 2025 10:51  
To: Oliver Robbins  
Cc: 
 ; Ailsa Terry (No.10) ; Dan York-Smith ; Simon Madden ; Ellen Atkinson ; Zoe 
Kinch (Cabinet Office) (Sensitive)  
Subject: Subject: Re: [Do not forward/official sensitive] Draft PM letter from Cab Sec  
Thanks Olly, I've put the dates in below, and added the penultimate paragraph as 
Chris was keen to give the PM assurances on the processes being carried out properly 
as far as he has seen. Let me know if you have strong views on that.  
 good to get confirmation from you asap on the conflicts of 
interests point. 
Let me know if there are further views by 11.30 if possible.  
Best,  
Elkie  
///  
Prime Minister  
In discussion with Foreign Office, Cabinet Office and No10 officials, I have looked into whether the 
correct process was followed for both the appointment of the previous HMA Washington, Peter 
Mandelson, and the process around your decision to withdraw him from post.  
The process for his appointment was unusual but not irregular. This was a direct Prime Ministerial 
appointment from outside either the Diplomatic or Home Civil Service. You received advice from my 
predecessor on 11 November 2024 before deciding on this course. That advice explained your right to 
Personal
Personal
Personal
Personal
JCS
JCS
JCS
JCS
Personal

Personal
Personal
JCS
JCS
JCS
JCS
115

make such an appointment and the process for doing so but did not give specific advice on candidates. 
Following that advice, the Propriety and Ethics Team (PET) conducted an open-source due diligence 
report on Lord Mandelson. This identified his relationship with Jeffrey Epstein as a reputational risk. 
Your special advisers then obtained further assurances from Lord Mandelson on this specific issue 
which I have not been sighted on. No evidence has come to light suggesting concerns were raised with 
you or my predecessor on national security grounds as part of the pre-appointment timeline. On 18 
December 2024 your office wrote to the previous Permanent Under-Secretary at the FCDO instructing 
him to seek The King's approval for the appointment, which was duly sought later the same day. The 
appointment was announced on 20 December 2024. The FCDO immediately commenced the national 
security vetting process, submitting Lord Mandelson as a priority candidate for Developed Vetting to 
the UK Security Vetting Agency. Clearance was duly given before he commenced his posting on 10 
February 2025. The FCDO also took on the task of working with Lord Mandelson to understand and 
mitigate conflicts of interest in advance of his departure for Washington. [This process was also 
concluded before his departure for Washington.]  
Your decision to withdraw Lord Mandelson as HMA Washington was taken in the light of your belief 
that his account of his relationship with Jeffrey Epstein at the time of appointment had been less than 
totally candid, and that his replies on questions posed by his line manager, the FCDO Permanent 
Under-Secretary, left open the possibility that further damaging revelations would ensue. The FCDO 
is now in correspondence with Lord Mandelson about the termination of his contract. [In both the 
appointment and the decision to withdraw I have seen no evidence that proper processes were not 
carried out adequately.]  
I will keep this under review if any further evidence emerges.  
Elkie Symes  
Principal Private Secretary to the Cabinet Secretary 
M: 
70 Whitehall, London, SW1A 2AS  
On Tue, 16 Sept 2025 at 10:31, Oliver Robbins 
 wrote: 
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE - HR - MANAGEMENT 
Elkie  
Apologies for missing your deadline. I would more precisely separate out the issues, 
explaining what was done and why it met a regularity test. My attempt below (though of 
course I still haven't seen many of the potentially crucial documents).  
Olly  
---  
Prime Minister  
In discussion with Foreign Office, Cabinet Office and No10 officials, I have looked into 
whether the correct process was followed for both the appointment of the previous HMA 
Washington, Peter Mandelson, and the process around your decision to withdraw him 
from post.  
The process for his appointment was unusual but not irregular. This was a direct Prime 
Ministerial appointment from outside either the Diplomatic or Home Civil Service. You 
received advice from my predecessor on [date] before deciding on this course. That advice 
explained [your right to make such and appointment and the pros and cons of doing so / the 
process for doing so / what?] but did not give specific advice on candidates. Your office 
subsequently commissioned an open-source due diligence report from the Cabinet Office's 
Propriety and Ethics Team ("PET") on Lord Mandelson. This identified his relationship with 
Jeffrey Epstein [and its long-running nature?] as a reputational risk. Your office obtained 
further assurances from Lord Mandelson on this specific issue. No evidence has come to 
light suggesting concerns were raised with you or my predecessor on national security 
Personal
Personal
Personal
Personal
116


grounds as part of the pre-appointment timeline. On [date] your office wrote to the previous 
Permanent Under-Secretary at the FCDO instructing him to seek The King's approval, which 
was duly sought later the same day.  
The appointment was announced on 20 December 2024. The FCDO immediately 
commenced the national security vetting process, submitting Lord Mandelson as a priority 
candidate for Developed Vetting to the UK Security Vetting Agency. Clearance was duly 
given before he commenced his posting on [date]. The FCDO also took on the task of 
working with Lord Mandelson to understand and mitigate and conflicts of interest. This 
process was also concluded before his departure for Washington [can 
confirm that's true - obv the discussion on his GC shares rumbled on forever but I think I had 
agreed mitigations with him before he started?].  
Your decision to withdraw Lord Mandelson as HMA Washington was taken in the light of 
your belief that his account of his relationship with Jeffrey Epstein at the time of appointment 
had been less than totally candid, and that his replies on questions posed by his line 
manager, the FCDO Permanent Under-Secretary, left open the possibility that further 
damaging revelations would ensue. The FCDO is now in correspondence with Lord 
Mandelson about the termination of his contract.  
I will keep this under review if any further evidence emerges.  
CW

On Mon, 15 Sept 2025 at 18:56, Elkie Symes < 
 wrote:  
[DO NOT FORWARD]  
Hi all,  
Cab Sec would like to send the PM a note tomorrow setting out his views on the 
process that was taken for the appointment of the former HMA Washington. 
Grateful if I could get views on the draft below by 10am / confirmation 
No10/FCDO are content. Note: Cab Sec has not cleared the draft below.  
Best,  
Elkie

///  
[TOP]  
Dear Prime Minister, 
In discussion with Foreign Office, Cabinet Office and No10 officials, I have looked into whether 
the correct process was followed for both the appointment of HMA Washington, Peter 
Mandelson, and the process around your decision to withdraw him from post. As you know this 
was a political diplomatic appointment, and therefore - as set out in the note from my 
predecessor, which you received before making this appointment - requires the necessary 
security clearances and doing due diligence on any potential Conflicts of Interest or other issues 
of which you should be aware.  
In both the appointment and the withdrawal, I have seen no evidence that proper processes were 
not carried out adequately. In my discussions with officials I have reviewed or discussed: - the 
Propriety and Ethics open source due diligence that you saw as part of the appointment process;  
- the post appointment vetting which was completed post announcement but before he took up
post; and
- your decision to withdraw HMA Washington once the content of the emails was known and
HMA Washington was given the right to reply to his line manager, Oliver Robbins (FCDO
Permanent Under Secretary)
I will keep this under review if any further evidence emerges.
[TAIL]
Elkie Symes
Principal Private Secretary to the Cabinet Secretary
M: 
70 Whitehall, London, SW1A 2AS
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE - HR - MANAGEMENT

OFFICIAL – SENSITIVE - PERSONAL 
OFFICIAL – SENSITIVE PERSONAL 
From the Cabinet Secretary 
16 September 2025 
PRIME MINISTER 
APPOINTMENT AND WITHDRAWAL FROM POST OF HMA 
WASHINGTON 
As we discussed, I have looked into whether the correct process was followed for both 
the appointment of the previous HMA Washington, Peter Mandelson, and the process 
around your decision to withdraw him from post. 
The process for his appointment was unusual but not irregular. This was a direct 
Prime Ministerial appointment from outside both the Diplomatic and Home Civil 
Service. You received advice from my predecessor on 11 November 2024 before 
deciding on this type of appointment. That advice explained your right to make such 
an appointment and the process for doing so but did not give specific advice on 
candidates.  The process that took place followed the advice my predecessor gave in 
particular “for them to acquire the necessary security clearances and do due diligence 
on any potential conflicts of interest or other issues of which you should be aware 
before confirming your choice.” All processes set out by my predecessor took place. 
Two actions, the security vetting and the process on conflicts of interest took place 
after the decision to appoint. I do not however consider that to be material as the 
vetting process was complete before the previous HMA Washington took up post on 
10 February 2025, and it is more usual for security vetting to happen after 
appointment. 
Following the advice from my predecessor, the Propriety and Ethics Team (PET) 
conducted an open-source due diligence report on Lord Mandelson. This identified his 
relationship with Jeffrey Epstein as a reputational risk.  Your special advisers then 
obtained further assurances from Lord Mandelson on this specific issue. No evidence 
has come to light suggesting concerns were raised with you or my predecessor on 
national security grounds as part of the pre-appointment timeline.  On 18 December 
2024 your office wrote to the previous Permanent Under-Secretary at the FCDO

parsed.body:
In discussion with Foreign Office, Cabinet Office and No10 officials, I have looked into whether 
the correct process was followed for both the appointment of HMA Washington, Peter 
Mandelson, and the process around your decision to withdraw him from post. As you know this 
was a political diplomatic appointment, and therefore - as set out in the note from my 
predecessor, which you received before making this appointment - requires the necessary 
security clearances and doing due diligence on any potential Conflicts of Interest or other issues 
of which you should be aware.  
In both the appointment and the withdrawal, I have seen no evidence that proper processes were 
not carried out adequately. In my discussions with officials I have reviewed or discussed: - the 
Propriety and Ethics open source due diligence that you saw as part of the appointment process;  
- the post appointment vetting which was completed post announcement but before he took up
post; and
- your decision to withdraw HMA Washington once the content of the emails was known and
HMA Washington was given the right to reply to his line manager, Oliver Robbins (FCDO
Permanent Under Secretary)
I will keep this under review if any further evidence emerges.
[TAIL]
Elkie Symes
Principal Private Secretary to the Cabinet Secretary
M: 
70 Whitehall, London, SW1A 2AS
